name: Rebel Hours Return to the University Libraries for Finals Week

name: Student Health Center and FAST Center Reach Perfect Score for Re-Accreditation
summary: The UNLV Student Health Center and Faculty and Staff Treatment Center recently achieved a perfect score in a national reaccreditation survey by the Accreditation Association for Ambulatory Health Care . During a rigorous two-day on-site survey this week, expert AAAHC...

name: Action Required: Sophos Expires May 15, Check Your Security Software
summary: Our Sophos license will end on May 15 and will stop receiving updates that protect your computer. Instead, your campus computer should now be running SentinelOne , a modern, AI-driven security platform designed to better protect against today's sophisticated cyber threats.

name: Meet with a TIAA Representative on Campus May 13 & 15
summary: Meet with a TIAA Financial Consultant at the CSB Wednesday, May 13 or Friday, May 15 to help create a financial plan for your future. Discuss investments, saving, and withdrawing from your plan when you retire. All employees are welcome to meet with a consultant. Registration...

name: Are You Pregnant? We’d Love Your Help with Our Research!
summary: We're currently looking to recruit individuals who are currently pregnant with a singleton pregnancy (one child). Participants must be 18 years or older and have no contraindication to daily-living-level activity in pregnancy. This research aims to characterize physical...

name: Classified Employee of the Month Nominations
summary: Nomination Process If you know a classified employee who deserves an opportunity at this noteworthy award, please submit your nominations by Friday, May 22.

name: UNLV Announces 2026–27 Common Read Selection
summary: A committee of faculty, staff, and students has selected The Anthropocene Reviewed: Essays on a Human-Centered Planet by John Green as UNLV’s common read for the 2026–27 academic year. Incoming students will receive a copy of the book during Rebel Ready Week. A former New...
